# oms-work — LLM Reference

Execution engine for cleared tasks. Runs queued tasks in isolated git worktrees, validates via chain, commits on pass, leaves branch open on stop.

## Triggers — use /oms-work when

- cleared-queue.md has tasks with Status: queued
- Discord `/work` received in a project channel
- After /oms session generates queued tasks

Skip /oms-work when:
- Queue is empty or all tasks are done/cto-stop
- cto-stop tasks need re-spec — resolve at next /oms session first
- Task scope is unclear — run /oms to elaborate first

## Invocation

```
/oms-work              # auto-detect project from cwd
/oms-work <slug>       # explicit project slug (from oms-config.json)
```

## What it does

1. Reads cleared-queue.md — identifies ready vs blocked tasks
2. Launches one subagent per ready task (parallel for independent, serial for chains)
3. Each subagent: completes the task → runs validation chain → writes quality record
4. Commits done tasks to `oms-work/task-nnn` branch; leaves cto-stop branches open
5. Posts task status to Discord milestone thread
6. After all tasks: invokes Executive Briefing Agent

## Validation chains

| Task type | Chain |
|---|---|
| Engineering | dev → qa → em |
| Research | researcher → cro → cpo |
| Infra-critical | dev → cto |

## Quality record

Each completed task writes to `~/.claude/oms-costs/<slug>-<task-id>.json`:
passed, fail_at, validators dict, notes. Also appends to `[project]/.claude/oms-metrics.json`.

## Key files

Read: cleared-queue.md, oms-config.json, task Context: files
Write: cleared-queue.md (status), oms-briefing.md, oms-daily-log.md, oms-metrics.json, oms-costs/

## What it doesn't do

- Never re-discusses or replans — that's /oms
- Never merges to main — branches stay open for CEO to merge
- Never runs cto-stop tasks — those need re-spec first
